title: Turn Any Workflow Into a One-Step “Skill”
summary: Keith AI presents a method for abstracting complex workflows into single-step callable skills. This approach enables automation and reusability of multi-step processes as atomic operations.
writeup: A brief demonstration of a "skills" feature that lets a user save a completed multi-step workflow, including the tone used, API connections, and sequencing, under a single named shortcut. The core proposition is that recurring workflows collapse into one callable step rather than being rebuilt each time, and that stored skills can improve incrementally with use.
key_points: A completed workflow can be saved as a named skill, capturing the full process including tone preferences and social media API connection details, so repeating it requires no reconstruction.
Calling the skill next time runs the entire multi-step sequence in a single invocation, eliminating the need to re-specify each step.
Skills are described as improving over time, implying the stored workflow is not static but can be refined through repeated use.
